payload.outputSummary:
---
name: push
description:
  Push current branch changes to origin and create or update the corresponding
  pull request; use when asked to push, publish updates, or create pull request.
---

# Push

## Prerequisites

- `gh` CLI is installed and available in `PATH`.
- `gh auth status` succeeds for GitHub operations in this repo.

## Goals

- Push current branch changes to `origin` safely.
- Create a PR if none exists for the branch, otherwise update the existing PR.
- Keep branch history clean when remote has moved.

## Related Skills

- `pull`: use this when push is rejected or sync is not clean (non-fast-forward,
  merge conflict risk, or stale branch).

## Steps

1. Identify current branch and confirm remote state.
2. Run local validation (`make -C elixir all`) before pushing.
3. Push branch to `origin` with upstream tracking if needed, using whatever
   remote URL is already configured.
4. If push is not clean/rejected:
   - If the failure is a non-fast-forward or sync problem, run the `pull`
     skill to merge `origin/main`, resolve conflicts, and rerun validation.
   - Push again; use `--force-with-lease` only when history was rewritten.
   - If the failure is due to auth, permissions, or workflow restrictions on
     the configured remote, stop and surface the exact error instead of
     rewriting remotes or switching protocols as a workaround.

5. Ensure a PR exists for the branch:
   - If no PR exists, create one.
   - If a PR exists and is open, update it.
   - If branch is tied to a closed/merged PR, create a new branch + PR.
   - Write a proper PR title that clearly describes the change outcome
   - For branch updates, explicitly reconsider whether current PR title still
     matches the latest scope; update it if it no longer does.
6. Write/update PR body explicitly using `.github/pull_request_template.md`:
   - Fill every section with concrete content for this change.
   - Replace all placeholder comments (`<!-- ... -->`).
   - Keep bullets/checkboxe

diff.content:
diff --git a//tmp/symphony-15-commit-message.txt b//tmp/symphony-15-commit-message.txt
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..9eb03d4dabc24427a96003e4234906ab93068735
--- /dev/null
+++ b//tmp/symphony-15-commit-message.txt
@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+fix(audit): recover canonicalization rejections
+
+Summary:
+- classify canonicalization failures as recoverable audit errors
+- rebase rejected pending batches without changing durable identities
+- pin ANSI canonical bytes to the Python RFC 8785 fixture hash
+
+Rationale:
+- canonicalization failures require the same bounded repair as hash and
+  chain rejections so legacy pending output can drain safely
+
+Tests:
+- make -C elixir all
+
+Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
diff --git a/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/audit/outbox.ex b/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/audit/outbox.ex
index 43673349e7342df7e5abdc04e40a9e5940ce5fb5..ebbe81670124a5b65045d1680a28388f44bb84a2
--- a/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/audit/outbox.ex
+++ b/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/audit/outbox.ex
@@ -234,6 +234,7 @@
        )
        when status in [409, 422] and
               code in [
+                "audit_canonicalization_failed",
                 "audit_chain_conflict",
                 "audit_chain_start_invalid",
                 "audit_event_hash_invalid",
diff --git a/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/game_api/client.ex b/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/game_api/client.ex
index 935631300cf9e9d35634b209b78a81b6e71e300f..c15565aa0d7aedc3972581cc7584767361bc34fe
--- a/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/game_api/client.ex
+++ b/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/game_api/client.ex
@@ -11,6 +11,7 @@
   alias SymphonyElixir.Tracker.Issue
 
   @recoverable_audit_error_codes ~w(
+    audit_canonicalization_failed
     audit_chain_conflict
     audit_chain_start_invalid
     audit_event_hash_invalid
diff --git a/elixir/test/symphony_elixir/audit/canonical_json_test.exs b/elixir/test/symphony_elixir/audit/canonical_json_test.exs
index d8df7bdc29250e19a7719fbd3271b5d3e595cfe8..0b86e395cc44a415786b66ee573cc3ed3c938690
--- a/elixir/test/symphony_elixir/audit/canonical_json_test.exs
+++ b/elixir/test/symphony_elixir/audit/canonical_json_test.exs
@@ -34,6 +34,18 @@
     assert canonical == ~S({"output":"\u001b\u0000\u001f\b\t\n\f\r"})
   end
 
+  test "matches the Python rfc8785 ANSI fixture hash" do
+    canonical =
+      %{"outputSummary" => "\e[0;32mpassed\e[0m"}
+      |> CanonicalJson.encode()
+      |> IO.iodata_to_binary()
+
+    assert canonical == ~S({"outputSummary":"\u001b[0;32mpassed\u001b[0m"})
+
+    assert Base.encode16(:crypto.hash(:sha256, canonical), case: :lower) ==
+             "09fe4ade10659bdf95036a8ccab87866e39ec58c8d00e628d3a6ecadf2e67d8e"
+  end
+
   test "sorts object properties by UTF-16 code units" do
     canonical = %{<<0xE000::utf8>> => 1, "😀" => 2} |> CanonicalJson.encode() |> IO.iodata_to_binary()
     assert canonical == ~s({"😀":2,"":1})
diff --git a/elixir/test/symphony_elixir/game_api_adapter_test.exs b/elixir/test/symphony_elixir/game_api_adapter_test.exs
index 49d52cd698e764d030700f2b03a1b6301e40ac26..ccc5b45fdd3a2ddc08eec0f90285e30a61aeaa52
--- a/elixir/test/symphony_elixir/game_api_adapter_test.exs
+++ b/elixir/test/symphony_elixir/game_api_adapter_test.exs
@@ -192,6 +192,10 @@
              "detail" => %{"error" => "audit_event_hash_invalid", "message" => "stale payload"}
            }) == {:game_api_http_error, 422, "audit_event_hash_invalid"}
 
+    assert Client.http_error(422, %{
+             "detail" => %{"error" => "audit_canonicalization_failed", "message" => "invalid bytes"}
+           }) == {:game_api_http_error, 422, "audit_canonicalization_failed"}
+
     assert Client.http_error(
              422,
              ~s({"detail":{"error":"audit_event_hash_invalid","message":"stale payload"}})

diff.content:
diff --git a//tmp/symphony-15-commit-message.txt b//tmp/symphony-15-commit-message.txt
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..9eb03d4dabc24427a96003e4234906ab93068735
--- /dev/null
+++ b//tmp/symphony-15-commit-message.txt
@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+fix(audit): recover canonicalization rejections
+
+Summary:
+- classify canonicalization failures as recoverable audit errors
+- rebase rejected pending batches without changing durable identities
+- pin ANSI canonical bytes to the Python RFC 8785 fixture hash
+
+Rationale:
+- canonicalization failures require the same bounded repair as hash and
+  chain rejections so legacy pending output can drain safely
+
+Tests:
+- make -C elixir all
+
+Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
diff --git a//tmp/symphony-15-pr-body.md b//tmp/symphony-15-pr-body.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..e4fd5908441950eac5615876aa2dff4e5e0d43d8
--- /dev/null
+++ b//tmp/symphony-15-pr-body.md
@@ -0,0 +1,22 @@
+#### Context
+
+ANSI command output exposed non-RFC 8785 control escapes and permanently rejected audit batches must be repaired safely.
+
+#### TL;DR
+
+*Canonicalize ANSI audit bytes and recover rejected pending chains without changing durable event identities.*
+
+#### Summary
+
+- Treat audit canonicalization failures as recoverable gateway responses.
+- Atomically rebase and rehash only unconfirmed events while preserving event and operation IDs.
+- Pin ANSI control bytes and SHA-256 output to the Python RFC 8785 fixture.
+
+#### Alternatives
+
+- Retrying unchanged batches cannot recover deterministic hash rejection and leaves later events blocked.
+
+#### Test Plan
+
+- [x] `make -C elixir all`
+- [x] Focused canonical JSON, outbox recovery, and game-api adapter tests (37 tests).

               code in [

                 "audit_chain_start_invalid",
                 "audit_event_hash_invalid",

     audit_chain_start_invalid
     audit_event_hash_invalid

   end

+    canonical =

+      |> IO.iodata_to_binary()

     assert canonical == ~s({"😀":2,"":1})

@@ -34,6 +34,18 @@
     assert canonical == ~S({"output":"\u001b\u0000\u001f\b\t\n\f\r"})
   end
 
+  test "matches the Python rfc8785 ANSI fixture hash" do
+    canonical =
+      %{"outputSummary" => "\e[0;32mpassed\e[0m"}
+      |> CanonicalJson.encode()
+      |> IO.iodata_to_binary()
+
+    assert canonical == ~S({"outputSummary":"\u001b[0;32mpassed\u001b[0m"})
+
+    assert Base.encode16(:crypto.hash(:sha256, canonical), case: :lower) ==
+             "09fe4ade10659bdf95036a8ccab87866e39ec58c8d00e628d3a6ecadf2e67d8e"
+  end
+
   test "sorts object properties by UTF-16 code units" do
     canonical = %{<<0xE000::utf8>> => 1, "😀" => 2} |> CanonicalJson.encode() |> IO.iodata_to_binary()

diff --git a//tmp/symphony-15-commit-message.txt b//tmp/symphony-15-commit-message.txt
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..9eb03d4dabc24427a96003e4234906ab93068735
--- /dev/null
+++ b//tmp/symphony-15-commit-message.txt
@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+fix(audit): recover canonicalization rejections
+
+Summary:
+- classify canonicalization failures as recoverable audit errors
+- rebase rejected pending batches without changing durable identities
+- pin ANSI canonical bytes to the Python RFC 8785 fixture hash
+
+Rationale:
+- canonicalization failures require the same bounded repair as hash and
+  chain rejections so legacy pending output can drain safely
+
+Tests:
+- make -C elixir all
+
+Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>
diff --git a//tmp/symphony-15-pr-body.md b//tmp/symphony-15-pr-body.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..e4fd5908441950eac5615876aa2dff4e5e0d43d8
--- /dev/null
+++ b//tmp/symphony-15-pr-body.md
@@ -0,0 +1,22 @@
+#### Context
+
+ANSI command output exposed non-RFC 8785 control escapes and permanently rejected audit batches must be repaired safely.
+
+#### TL;DR
+
+*Canonicalize ANSI audit bytes and recover rejected pending chains without changing durable event identities.*
+
+#### Summary
+
+- Treat audit canonicalization failures as recoverable gateway responses.
+- Atomically rebase and rehash only unconfirmed events while preserving event and operation IDs.
+- Pin ANSI control bytes and SHA-256 output to the Python RFC 8785 fixture.
+
+#### Alternatives
+
+- Retrying unchanged batches cannot recover deterministic hash rejection and leaves later events blocked.
+
+#### Test Plan
+
+- [x] `make -C elixir all`
+- [x] Focused canonical JSON, outbox recovery, and game-api adapter tests (37 tests).

payload.outputSummary:
end
    end
  end

  defp fetch_review_artifacts(context, attempt \\ 1) do
    native_ref = context.issue.native_ref || %{}

    case context.client.fetch_run_artifacts(
           native_ref["repositoryId"],
           native_ref["runId"],
           "reviews"
         ) do
      {:error, {:game_api_rate_limited, retry_after_ms}}
      when attempt < context.max_lookup_attempts and is_integer(retry_after_ms) and retry_after_ms >= 0 ->
        delay_ms = max(retry_after_ms, 50)

        Logger.warning(
          "Review lookup paused by the shared provider circuit; preserving the completed Codex stage " <>
            "attempt=#{attempt} delay_ms=#{delay_ms}"
        )

        context.sleep_fn.(delay_ms)
        fetch_review_artifacts(context, attempt + 1)

      result ->
        result
    end
  end

  defp run_repair(context, reviews, cycle) do
    findings = summarize_findings(reviews)

    prompt = """
    You are the BOS repair agent for #{context.issue.identifier}, repair cycle #{cycle}.
    Work only in the existing workspace and exact AgentRun. Review the durable
    findings below, inspect the current diff, implement every valid correction,
    run the complete repository validation, commit the repaired exact HEAD, and
    record updated evidence through bos-mcp. Do not approve your own work and do
    not request merge or human approval; fresh independent reviewers run next.

    Findings:
    #{findings}
    """

    run_role(
      context.app_server,
      context.workspace,
      context.issue,
      context.worker_host,
      "repair-agent",
      prompt,
      context.recipient
    )
  end

  defp run_finalizer(workspace, issue, recipient, worker_host, app_server) do
    prompt = """
    You are the BOS delivery coordinator for #{issue.identifier}. All five
    independent specialist Reviews have passed for the current repair cycle.
    Re-read the exact HEAD, acceptance contract, checks, reviews and evidence
    through bos-mcp. Ensure the P

diff --git a//tmp/symphony-15-pr-body.md b//tmp/symphony-15-pr-body.md
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..e4fd5908441950eac5615876aa2dff4e5e0d43d8
--- /dev/null
+++ b//tmp/symphony-15-pr-body.md
@@ -0,0 +1,22 @@
+#### Context
+
+ANSI command output exposed non-RFC 8785 control escapes and permanently rejected audit batches must be repaired safely.
+
+#### TL;DR
+
+*Canonicalize ANSI audit bytes and recover rejected pending chains without changing durable event identities.*
+
+#### Summary
+
+- Treat audit canonicalization failures as recoverable gateway responses.
+- Atomically rebase and rehash only unconfirmed events while preserving event and operation IDs.
+- Pin ANSI control bytes and SHA-256 output to the Python RFC 8785 fixture.
+
+#### Alternatives
+
+- Retrying unchanged batches cannot recover deterministic hash rejection and leaves later events blocked.
+
+#### Test Plan
+
+- [x] `make -C elixir all`
+- [x] Focused canonical JSON, outbox recovery, and game-api adapter tests (37 tests).
diff --git a/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/audit/outbox.ex b/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/audit/outbox.ex
index 43673349e7342df7e5abdc04e40a9e5940ce5fb5..ebbe81670124a5b65045d1680a28388f44bb84a2
--- a/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/audit/outbox.ex
+++ b/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/audit/outbox.ex
@@ -234,6 +234,7 @@
        )
        when status in [409, 422] and
               code in [
+                "audit_canonicalization_failed",
                 "audit_chain_conflict",
                 "audit_chain_start_invalid",
                 "audit_event_hash_invalid",
diff --git a/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/game_api/client.ex b/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/game_api/client.ex
index 935631300cf9e9d35634b209b78a81b6e71e300f..c15565aa0d7aedc3972581cc7584767361bc34fe
--- a/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/game_api/client.ex
+++ b/elixir/lib/symphony_elixir/game_api/client.ex
@@ -11,6 +11,7 @@
   alias SymphonyElixir.Tracker.Issue
 
   @recoverable_audit_error_codes ~w(
+    audit_canonicalization_failed
     audit_chain_conflict
     audit_chain_start_invalid
     audit_event_hash_invalid
diff --git a/elixir/test/symphony_elixir/audit/canonical_json_test.exs b/elixir/test/symphony_elixir/audit/canonical_json_test.exs
index d8df7bdc29250e19a7719fbd3271b5d3e595cfe8..0b86e395cc44a415786b66ee573cc3ed3c938690
--- a/elixir/test/symphony_elixir/audit/canonical_json_test.exs
+++ b/elixir/test/symphony_elixir/audit/canonical_json_test.exs
@@ -34,6 +34,18 @@
     assert canonical == ~S({"output":"\u001b\u0000\u001f\b\t\n\f\r"})
   end
 
+  test "matches the Python rfc8785 ANSI fixture hash" do
+    canonical =
+      %{"outputSummary" => "\e[0;32mpassed\e[0m"}
+      |> CanonicalJson.encode()
+      |> IO.iodata_to_binary()
+
+    assert canonical == ~S({"outputSummary":"\u001b[0;32mpassed\u001b[0m"})
+
+    assert Base.encode16(:crypto.hash(:sha256, canonical), case: :lower) ==
+             "09fe4ade10659bdf95036a8ccab87866e39ec58c8d00e628d3a6ecadf2e67d8e"
+  end
+
   test "sorts object properties by UTF-16 code units" do
     canonical = %{<<0xE000::utf8>> => 1, "😀" => 2} |> CanonicalJson.encode() |> IO.iodata_to_binary()
     assert canonical == ~s({"😀":2,"":1})
diff --git a/elixir/test/symphony_elixir/game_api_adapter_test.exs b/elixir/test/symphony_elixir/game_api_adapter_test.exs
index 49d52cd698e764d030700f2b03a1b6301e40ac26..ccc5b45fdd3a2ddc08eec0f90285e30a61aeaa52
--- a/elixir/test/symphony_elixir/game_api_adapter_test.exs
+++ b/elixir/test/symphony_elixir/game_api_adapter_test.exs
@@ -192,6 +192,10 @@
              "detail" => %{"error" => "audit_event_hash_invalid", "message" => "stale payload"}
            }) == {:game_api_http_error, 422, "audit_event_hash_invalid"}
 
+    assert Client.http_error(422, %{
+             "detail" => %{"error" => "audit_canonicalization_failed", "message" => "invalid bytes"}
+           }) == {:game_api_http_error, 422, "audit_canonicalization_failed"}
+
     assert Client.http_error(
              422,
              ~s({"detail":{"error":"audit_event_hash_invalid","message":"stale payload"}})

diff --git a//tmp/symphony-15-commit-message.txt b//tmp/symphony-15-commit-message.txt
new file mode 100644
index 0000000000000000000000000000000000000000..9eb03d4dabc24427a96003e4234906ab93068735
--- /dev/null
+++ b//tmp/symphony-15-commit-message.txt
@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+fix(audit): recover canonicalization rejections
+
+Summary:
+- classify canonicalization failures as recoverable audit errors
+- rebase rejected pending batches without changing durable identities
+- pin ANSI canonical bytes to the Python RFC 8785 fixture hash
+
+Rationale:
+- canonicalization failures require the same bounded repair as hash and
+  chain rejections so legacy pending output can drain safely
+
+Tests:
+- make -C elixir all
+
+Co-authored-by: Codex <codex@openai.com>

+
+#### Test Plan

+- [x] `make -C elixir all`
+- [x] Focused canonical JSON, outbox recovery, and game-api adapter tests (37 tests).
